{{Short description|Group of kofun burial mounds in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | native_name = 中郷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mukaideyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mukaideyama Kofun 1 | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|38|08|N|136|05|09|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakagō Kofun Cluster'''|中郷古墳群|Nakagō Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Sakashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuruga, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205293 |title= 中郷古墳群 |trans-title= Nakago kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Nakagō Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside east of the centre of the city of Tsuruga facing the [[Sea of Japan]], and consists of two separate groups: the {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster'''|向出山古墳群|Mukaiyama Kofun-gun}} and the {{nihongo|'''Myōjinyama Kofun Cluster'''|明神山古墳群|Myōjinyama Kofun-gun}}. These tumuli date from the 4th to 6th century AD and were discovered during the construction of a highway bypass connecting the [[Hokuriku Expressway]] with [[Japan National Route 8]], during which time a number of other archaeological discoveries were made, including the Yoshikawa Site, a [[Yayoi period]] settlement, and the Kotanigahora Kofun Cluster; however, neither of these sites received government protection and were totally destroyed during highway construction after a hasty [[Rescue archaeology|compliance excavation]]. The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[burial chamber]]s were opened. [[Grave goods]] found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, [[bronze mirror]]s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from [[Tsuruga Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large burial mound from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: [[Zenpokoenfun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: [[Zenpokoenfun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}

{{Short description|Group of kofun burial mounds in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | native_name = 中郷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mukaideyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mukaideyama Kofun 1 | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|38|08|N|136|05|09|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakagō Kofun Cluster'''|中郷古墳群|Nakagō Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Sakashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuruga, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205293 |title= 中郷古墳群 |trans-title= Nakago kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Nakagō Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside east of the centre of the city of Tsuruga facing the [[Sea of Japan]], and consists of two separate groups: the {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster'''|向出山古墳群|Mukaiyama Kofun-gun}} and the {{nihongo|'''Myōjinyama Kofun Cluster'''|明神山古墳群|Myōjinyama Kofun-gun}}. These tumuli date from the 4th to 6th century AD and were discovered during the construction of a highway bypass connecting the [[Hokuriku Expressway]] with [[Japan National Route 8]], during which time a number of other archaeological discoveries were made, including the Yoshikawa Site, a [[Yayoi period]] settlement, and the Kotanigahora Kofun Cluster; however, neither of these sites received government protection and were totally destroyed during highway construction after a hasty [[Rescue archaeology|compliance excavation]]. The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[burial chamber]]s were opened. [[Grave goods]] found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, [[bronze mirror]]s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from [[Tsuruga Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large burial mound from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: [[Zenpokoenfun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.

* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: [[Zenpokoenfun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}

{{Short description|Group of kofun burial mounds in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | native_name = 中郷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mukaideyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mukaideyama Kofun 1 | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|38|08|N|136|05|09|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakagō Kofun Cluster'''|中郷古墳群|Nakagō Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Sakashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuruga, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205293 |title= 中郷古墳群 |trans-title= Nakago kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Nakagō Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside east of the centre of the city of Tsuruga facing the [[Sea of Japan]], and consists of two separate groups: the {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster'''|向出山古墳群|Mukaiyama Kofun-gun}} and the {{nihongo|'''Myōjinyama Kofun Cluster'''|明神山古墳群|Myōjinyama Kofun-gun}}. These tumuli date from the 4th to 6th century AD and were discovered during the construction of a highway bypass connecting the [[Hokuriku Expressway]] with [[Japan National Route 8]], during which time a number of other archaeological discoveries were made, including the Yoshikawa Site, a [[Yayoi period]] settlement, and the Kotanigahora Kofun Cluster; however, neither of these sites received government protection and were totally destroyed during highway construction after a hasty [[Rescue archaeology|compliance excavation]]. The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[burial chamber]]s were opened. [[Grave goods]] found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, [[bronze mirror]]s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from [[Tsuruga Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large burial mound from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: [[Zenpokoenfun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.

* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: [[Zenpokoenfun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

{{Short description|Group of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | native_name = 中郷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mukaideyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mukaideyama Kofun 1 | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|38|08|N|136|05|09|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakagō Kofun Cluster'''|中郷古墳群|Nakagō Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Sakashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuruga, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205293 |title= 中郷古墳群 |trans-title= Nakago kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Nakagō Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside east of the centre of the city of Tsuruga facing the [[Sea of Japan]], and consists of two separate groups: the {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster'''|向出山古墳群|Mukaiyama Kofun-gun}} and the {{nihongo|'''Myōjinyama Kofun Cluster'''|明神山古墳群|Myōjinyama Kofun-gun}}. These tumuli date from the 4th to 6th century AD and were discovered during the construction of a highway bypass connecting the [[Hokuriku Expressway]] with [[Japan National Route 8]], during which time a number of other archaeological discoveries were made, including the Yoshikawa Site, a [[Yayoi period]] settlement, and the Kotanigahora Kofun Cluster; however, neither of these sites received government protection and were totally destroyed during highway construction after a hasty [[Rescue archaeology|compliance excavation]]. The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[burial chamber]]s were opened. [[Grave goods]] found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, [[bronze mirror]]s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from [[Tsuruga Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large Kofun from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: [[Zenpokoenfun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: [[Zenpokoenfun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Group of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | native_name = 中郷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mukaideyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mukaideyama Kofun 1 | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|38|08|N|136|05|09|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakagō Kofun Cluster'''|中郷古墳群|Nakagō Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Sakashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuruga, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205293 |title= 中郷古墳群 |trans-title= Nakago kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Nakagō Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside east of the centre of the city of Tsuruga facing the [[Sea of Japan]], and consists of two separate groups: the {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster'''|向出山古墳群|Mukaiyama Kofun-gun}} and the {{nihongo|'''Myōjinyama Kofun Cluster'''|明神山古墳群|Myōjinyama Kofun-gun}}. These tumuli date from the 4th to 6th century AD and were discovered during the construction of a highway bypass connecting the [[Hokuriku Expressway]] with [[Japan National Route 8]], during which time a number of other archaeological discoveries were made, including the Yoshikawa Site, a [[Yayoi period]] settlement, and the Kotanigahora Kofun Cluster; however, neither of these sites received government protection and were totally destroyed during highway construction after a hasty [[Rescue archaeology|compliance excavation]]. The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[burial chamber]]s were opened. [[Grave goods]] found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, [[bronze mirror]]s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from [[Tsuruga Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large Kofun from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: [[Zenpokoenfun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group}} *[http://www.city.tsuruga.lg.jp/about_city/news_from_facility/gaibu_shisetsu/hakubutsukan/past_exhibactivities/kaisetusito.files/001-bs02-mukaideyama.pdf Tsuruga city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/tsuruga-nakago.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: [[Zenpokoenfun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

{{Short description|Group of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | native_name = 中郷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mukaideyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mukaideyama Kofun 1 | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|38|08|N|136|05|09|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakagō Kofun Cluster'''|中郷古墳群|Nakagō Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Sakashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuruga, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205293 |title= 中郷古墳群 |trans-title= Nakago kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Nakagō Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside east of the centre of the city of Tsuruga facing the [[Sea of Japan]], and consists of two separate groups: the {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster'''|向出山古墳群|Mukaiyama Kofun-gun}} and the {{nihongo|'''Myōjinyama Kofun Cluster'''|明神山古墳群|Myōjinyama Kofun-gun}}. These tumuli date from the 4th to 6th century AD and were discovered during the construction of a highway bypass connecting the [[Hokuriku Expressway]] with [[Japan National Route 8]], during which time a number of other archaeological discoveries were made, including the Yoshikawa Site, a [[Yayoi period]] settlement, and the Kotanigahora Kofun Cluster; however, neither of these sites received government protection and were totally destroyed during highway construction after a hasty [[Rescue archaeology|compliance excavation]]. The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[burial chamber]]s were opened. [[Grave goods]] found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, [[bronze mirror]]s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from [[Tsuruga Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large Kofun from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: [[Zenpokoenfun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group}} *[http://www.city.tsuruga.lg.jp/about_city/news_from_facility/gaibu_shisetsu/hakubutsukan/past_exhibactivities/kaisetusito.files/001-bs02-mukaideyama.pdf Tsuruga city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/tsuruga-nakago.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.

* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: [[Zenpokoenfun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}

{{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | native_name = 中郷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mukaideyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mukaideyama Kofun 1 | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|38|08|N|136|05|09|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakagō Kofun Cluster'''|中郷古墳群|Nakagō Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Sakashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuruga, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205293 |title= 中郷古墳群 |trans-title= Nakago kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Nakagō Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside east of the centre of the city of Tsuruga facing the [[Sea of Japan]], and consists of two separate groups: the {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster'''|向出山古墳群|Mukaiyama Kofun-gun}} and the {{nihongo|'''Myōjinyama Kofun Cluster'''|明神山古墳群|Myōjinyama Kofun-gun}}. These tumuli date from the 4th to 6th century AD and were discovered during the construction of a highway bypass connecting the [[Hokuriku Expressway]] with [[Japan National Route 8]], during which time a number of other archaeological discoveries were made, including the Yoshikawa Site, a [[Yayoi period]] settlement, and the Kotanigahora Kofun Cluster; however, neither of these sites received government protection and were totally destroyed during highway construction after a hasty [[Rescue archaeology|compliance excavation]]. The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[burial chamber]]s were opened. [[Grave goods]] found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, [[bronze mirror]]s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from [[Tsuruga Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large Kofun from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: [[Zenpokoenfun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: [[Zenpokoenfun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}

{{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | native_name = 中郷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mukaideyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mukaideyama Kofun 1 | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|38|08|N|136|05|09|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakagō Kofun Cluster'''|中郷古墳群|Nakagō Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Sakashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuruga, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205293 |title= 中郷古墳群 |trans-title= Nakago kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Nakagō Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside east of the centre of the city of Tsuruga facing the [[Sea of Japan]], and consists of two separate groups: the {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster'''|向出山古墳群|Mukaiyama Kofun-gun}} and the {{nihongo|'''Myōjinyama Kofun Cluster'''|明神山古墳群|Myōjinyama Kofun-gun}}. These tumuli date from the 4th to 6th century AD and were discovered during the construction of a highway bypass connecting the [[Hokuriku Expressway]] with [[Japan National Route 8]], during which time a number of other archaeological discoveries were made, including the Yoshikawa Site, a [[Yayoi period]] settlement, and the Kotanigahora Kofun Cluster; however, neither of these sites received government protection and were totally destroyed during highway construction after a hasty [[Rescue archaeology|compliance excavation]]. The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[burial chamber]]s were opened. [[Grave goods]] found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, [[bronze mirror]]s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from [[Tsuruga Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large Kofun from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: [[Zenpokoenfun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.

* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: [[Zenpokoenfun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group}} *[http://www.city.tsuruga.lg.jp/about_city/news_from_facility/gaibu_shisetsu/hakubutsukan/past_exhibactivities/kaisetusito.files/001-bs02-mukaideyama.pdf Tsuruga city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/tsuruga-nakago.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
{{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | native_name = 中郷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mukaideyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mukaideyama Kofun 1 | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|38|08|N|136|05|09|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakagō Kofun Cluster'''|中郷古墳群|Nakagō Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Sakashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuruga, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205293 |title= 中郷古墳群 |trans-title= Nakago kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Nakagō Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside east of the centre of the city of Tsuruga facing the [[Sea of Japan]], and consists of two separate groups: the {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster'''|向出山古墳群|Mukaiyama Kofun-gun}} and the {{nihongo|'''Myōjinyama Kofun Cluster'''|明神山古墳群|Myōjinyama Kofun-gun}}. These tumuli date from the 4th to 6th century AD and were discovered during the construction of a highway bypass connecting the [[Hokuriku Expressway]] with [[Japan National Route 8]], during which time a number of other archaeological discoveries were made, including the Yoshikawa Site, a [[Yayoi period]] settlement, and the Kotanigahora Kofun Cluster; however, neither of these sites received government protection and were totally destroyed during highway construction after a hasty [[Rescue archaeology|compliance excavation]]. The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[burial chamber]]s were opened. [[Grave goods]] found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, [[bronze mirror]]s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from [[Tsuruga Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large Kofun from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: [[Zenpokoenfun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group}} *[http://www.city.tsuruga.lg.jp/about_city/news_from_facility/gaibu_shisetsu/hakubutsukan/past_exhibactivities/kaisetusito.files/001-bs02-mukaideyama.pdf Tsuruga city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/tsuruga-nakago.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Echizen Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakagō Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}}

==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group}} *[http://www.city.tsuruga.lg.jp/about_city/news_from_facility/gaibu_shisetsu/hakubutsukan/past_exhibactivities/kaisetusito.files/001-bs02-mukaideyama.pdf Tsuruga city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/tsuruga-nakago.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Echizen Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]] {{citation needed|Nakagō Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]
[[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | native_name = 中郷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mukaideyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mukaideyama Kofun 1 | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|38|08|N|136|05|09|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakagō Kofun Cluster'''|中郷古墳群|Nakagō Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Sakashita neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuruga, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205293 |title= 中郷古墳群 |trans-title= Nakago kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Nakagō Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside east of the centre of the city of Tsuruga facing the [[Sea of Japan]], and consists of two separate groups: the {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster'''|向出山古墳群|Mukaiyama Kofun-gun}} and the {{nihongo|'''Myōjinyama Kofun Cluster'''|明神山古墳群|Myōjinyama Kofun-gun}}. These tumuli date from the 4th to 6th century AD and were discovered during the construction of a highway bypass connecting the [[Hokuriku Expressway]] with [[Japan National Route 8]], during which time a number of other archaeological discoveries were made, including the Yoshikawa Site, a [[Yayoi period]] settlement, and the Kotanigahora Kofun Cluster; however, neither of these sites received government protection and were totally destroyed during highway construction after a hasty [[Rescue archaeology|compliance excavation]]. The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[burial chamber]]s were opened. [[Grave goods]] found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, [[bronze mirror]]s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from [[Tsuruga Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large Kofun from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: [[Zenpokoenfun|Keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group}} *[http://www.city.tsuruga.lg.jp/about_city/news_from_facility/gaibu_shisetsu/hakubutsukan/past_exhibactivities/kaisetusito.files/001-bs02-mukaideyama.pdf Tsuruga city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/tsuruga-nakago.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]]{{draft categories| [[Category:History of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Echizen Province]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]] {{citation needed|Nakagō Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]
[[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from [[Tsuruga Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
