<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group]][[en:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中郷古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group]][[en:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中郷古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group]][[en:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中郷古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28683739]] Wikidata name: Nakagō Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | native_name = 中郷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mukaideyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mukaideyama Kofun 1 | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|38|08|N|136|05|09|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.63555556|N|136.08583333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tsuruga, Fukui|en|Tsuruga, Fukui|WD=Q847581|lt=Tsuruga, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakagō Kofun Cluster'''|中郷古墳群|Nakagō Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Sakashita neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Tsuruga, Fukui|en|Tsuruga, Fukui|WD=Q847581|lt=Tsuruga, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205293 |title= 中郷古墳群 |trans-title= Nakago kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Nakagō Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside east of the centre of the city of Tsuruga facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}}, and consists of two separate groups: the {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster'''|向出山古墳群|Mukaiyama Kofun-gun}} and the {{nihongo|'''Myōjinyama Kofun Cluster'''|明神山古墳群|Myōjinyama Kofun-gun}}. These tumuli date from the 4th to 6th century AD and were discovered during the construction of a highway bypass connecting the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|en|Hokuriku Expressway|WD=Q1549512|lt=Hokuriku Expressway}} with {{ill|Japan National Route 8|en|Japan National Route 8|WD=Q640239|lt=Japan National Route 8}}, during which time a number of other archaeological discoveries were made, including the Yoshikawa Site, a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} settlement, and the Kotanigahora Kofun Cluster; however, neither of these sites received government protection and were totally destroyed during highway construction after a hasty {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|WD=Q653838|lt=compliance excavation}}. The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were opened. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Tsuruga Station|en|Tsuruga Station|WD=Q5364670|lt=Tsuruga Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|WD=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large Kofun from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Keyhole-shaped tumulus}} from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus}} from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|WD=Q10222682|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group}} *[http://www.city.tsuruga.lg.jp/about_city/news_from_facility/gaibu_shisetsu/hakubutsukan/past_exhibactivities/kaisetusito.files/001-bs02-mukaideyama.pdf Tsuruga city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/tsuruga-nakago.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Keyhole-shaped tumulus}} from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus}} from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were opened. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Tsuruga Station|en|Tsuruga Station|WD=Q5364670|lt=Tsuruga Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|WD=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large Kofun from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Keyhole-shaped tumulus}} from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus}} from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group]][[en:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中郷古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group]][[en:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中郷古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group]][[en:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中郷古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28683739]] Wikidata name: Nakagō Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | native_name = 中郷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mukaideyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mukaideyama Kofun 1 | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|38|08|N|136|05|09|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.63555556|N|136.08583333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tsuruga, Fukui|en|Tsuruga, Fukui|WD=Q847581|lt=Tsuruga, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakagō Kofun Cluster'''|中郷古墳群|Nakagō Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Sakashita neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Tsuruga, Fukui|en|Tsuruga, Fukui|WD=Q847581|lt=Tsuruga, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205293 |title= 中郷古墳群 |trans-title= Nakago kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Nakagō Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside east of the centre of the city of Tsuruga facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}}, and consists of two separate groups: the {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster'''|向出山古墳群|Mukaiyama Kofun-gun}} and the {{nihongo|'''Myōjinyama Kofun Cluster'''|明神山古墳群|Myōjinyama Kofun-gun}}. These tumuli date from the 4th to 6th century AD and were discovered during the construction of a highway bypass connecting the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|en|Hokuriku Expressway|WD=Q1549512|lt=Hokuriku Expressway}} with {{ill|Japan National Route 8|en|Japan National Route 8|WD=Q640239|lt=Japan National Route 8}}, during which time a number of other archaeological discoveries were made, including the Yoshikawa Site, a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} settlement, and the Kotanigahora Kofun Cluster; however, neither of these sites received government protection and were totally destroyed during highway construction after a hasty {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|WD=Q653838|lt=compliance excavation}}. The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were opened. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Tsuruga Station|en|Tsuruga Station|WD=Q5364670|lt=Tsuruga Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|WD=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large Kofun from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Keyhole-shaped tumulus}} from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus}} from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group]][[en:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中郷古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group]][[en:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中郷古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group]][[en:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中郷古墳群]]
{{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | native_name = 中郷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mukaideyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mukaideyama Kofun 1 | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|38|08|N|136|05|09|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.63555556|N|136.08583333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tsuruga, Fukui|en|Tsuruga, Fukui|WD=Q847581|lt=Tsuruga, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakagō Kofun Cluster'''|中郷古墳群|Nakagō Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Sakashita neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Tsuruga, Fukui|en|Tsuruga, Fukui|WD=Q847581|lt=Tsuruga, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205293 |title= 中郷古墳群 |trans-title= Nakago kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Nakagō Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside east of the centre of the city of Tsuruga facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}}, and consists of two separate groups: the {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster'''|向出山古墳群|Mukaiyama Kofun-gun}} and the {{nihongo|'''Myōjinyama Kofun Cluster'''|明神山古墳群|Myōjinyama Kofun-gun}}. These tumuli date from the 4th to 6th century AD and were discovered during the construction of a highway bypass connecting the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|en|Hokuriku Expressway|WD=Q1549512|lt=Hokuriku Expressway}} with {{ill|Japan National Route 8|en|Japan National Route 8|WD=Q640239|lt=Japan National Route 8}}, during which time a number of other archaeological discoveries were made, including the Yoshikawa Site, a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} settlement, and the Kotanigahora Kofun Cluster; however, neither of these sites received government protection and were totally destroyed during highway construction after a hasty {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|WD=Q653838|lt=compliance excavation}}. The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were opened. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Tsuruga Station|en|Tsuruga Station|WD=Q5364670|lt=Tsuruga Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|WD=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large Kofun from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Keyhole-shaped tumulus}} from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus}} from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|WD=Q10222682|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group}} *[http://www.city.tsuruga.lg.jp/about_city/news_from_facility/gaibu_shisetsu/hakubutsukan/past_exhibactivities/kaisetusito.files/001-bs02-mukaideyama.pdf Tsuruga city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/tsuruga-nakago.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were opened. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Tsuruga Station|en|Tsuruga Station|WD=Q5364670|lt=Tsuruga Station|1=Tsuruga Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|WD=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line|1=Hokuriku Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large Kofun from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Keyhole-shaped tumulus|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

{{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | native_name = 中郷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mukaideyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mukaideyama Kofun 1 | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|38|08|N|136|05|09|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.63555556|N|136.08583333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tsuruga, Fukui|en|Tsuruga, Fukui|WD=Q847581|lt=Tsuruga, Fukui|1=Tsuruga, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakagō Kofun Cluster'''|中郷古墳群|Nakagō Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Sakashita neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Tsuruga, Fukui|en|Tsuruga, Fukui|WD=Q847581|lt=Tsuruga, Fukui|1=Tsuruga, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205293 |title= 中郷古墳群 |trans-title= Nakago kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Nakagō Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside east of the centre of the city of Tsuruga facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}}, and consists of two separate groups: the {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster'''|向出山古墳群|Mukaiyama Kofun-gun}} and the {{nihongo|'''Myōjinyama Kofun Cluster'''|明神山古墳群|Myōjinyama Kofun-gun}}. These tumuli date from the 4th to 6th century AD and were discovered during the construction of a highway bypass connecting the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|en|Hokuriku Expressway|WD=Q1549512|lt=Hokuriku Expressway|1=Hokuriku Expressway}} with {{ill|Japan National Route 8|en|Japan National Route 8|WD=Q640239|lt=Japan National Route 8|1=Japan National Route 8}}, during which time a number of other archaeological discoveries were made, including the Yoshikawa Site, a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} settlement, and the Kotanigahora Kofun Cluster; however, neither of these sites received government protection and were totally destroyed during highway construction after a hasty {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|WD=Q653838|lt=compliance excavation|1=Rescue archaeology}}. The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were opened. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Tsuruga Station|en|Tsuruga Station|WD=Q5364670|lt=Tsuruga Station|1=Tsuruga Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|WD=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line|1=Hokuriku Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large Kofun from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Keyhole-shaped tumulus|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

[[en:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Mukaideyama Kofun Group}} [[ja:中郷古墳群]] {{wikidata link|Q28683739}} [[Category:Echizen Province]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Tsuruga, Fukui]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group]][[en:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中郷古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group]][[en:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中郷古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group]][[en:Nakagō Kofun Cluster]][[ja:中郷古墳群]]
{{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | native_name = 中郷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mukaideyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mukaideyama Kofun 1 | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|38|08|N|136|05|09|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.63555556|N|136.08583333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tsuruga, Fukui|en|Tsuruga, Fukui|WD=Q847581|lt=Tsuruga, Fukui|1=Tsuruga, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakagō Kofun Cluster'''|中郷古墳群|Nakagō Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Sakashita neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Tsuruga, Fukui|en|Tsuruga, Fukui|WD=Q847581|lt=Tsuruga, Fukui|1=Tsuruga, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205293 |title= 中郷古墳群 |trans-title= Nakago kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Nakagō Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside east of the centre of the city of Tsuruga facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}}, and consists of two separate groups: the {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster'''|向出山古墳群|Mukaiyama Kofun-gun}} and the {{nihongo|'''Myōjinyama Kofun Cluster'''|明神山古墳群|Myōjinyama Kofun-gun}}. These tumuli date from the 4th to 6th century AD and were discovered during the construction of a highway bypass connecting the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|en|Hokuriku Expressway|WD=Q1549512|lt=Hokuriku Expressway|1=Hokuriku Expressway}} with {{ill|Japan National Route 8|en|Japan National Route 8|WD=Q640239|lt=Japan National Route 8|1=Japan National Route 8}}, during which time a number of other archaeological discoveries were made, including the Yoshikawa Site, a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} settlement, and the Kotanigahora Kofun Cluster; however, neither of these sites received government protection and were totally destroyed during highway construction after a hasty {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|WD=Q653838|lt=compliance excavation|1=Rescue archaeology}}. The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were opened. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Tsuruga Station|en|Tsuruga Station|WD=Q5364670|lt=Tsuruga Station|1=Tsuruga Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|WD=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line|1=Hokuriku Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large Kofun from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Keyhole-shaped tumulus|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|WD=Q10222682|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)}}

{{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | native_name = 中郷古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Mukaideyama Kofun Group-1, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mukaideyama Kofun 1 | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakagō Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|38|08|N|136|05|09|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.63555556|N|136.08583333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tsuruga, Fukui|en|Tsuruga, Fukui|WD=Q847581|lt=Tsuruga, Fukui|1=Tsuruga, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakagō Kofun Cluster'''|中郷古墳群|Nakagō Kofun-gun}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Sakashita neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Tsuruga, Fukui|en|Tsuruga, Fukui|WD=Q847581|lt=Tsuruga, Fukui|1=Tsuruga, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205293 |title= 中郷古墳群 |trans-title= Nakago kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Nakagō Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside east of the centre of the city of Tsuruga facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}}, and consists of two separate groups: the {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster'''|向出山古墳群|Mukaiyama Kofun-gun}} and the {{nihongo|'''Myōjinyama Kofun Cluster'''|明神山古墳群|Myōjinyama Kofun-gun}}. These tumuli date from the 4th to 6th century AD and were discovered during the construction of a highway bypass connecting the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|en|Hokuriku Expressway|WD=Q1549512|lt=Hokuriku Expressway|1=Hokuriku Expressway}} with {{ill|Japan National Route 8|en|Japan National Route 8|WD=Q640239|lt=Japan National Route 8|1=Japan National Route 8}}, during which time a number of other archaeological discoveries were made, including the Yoshikawa Site, a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} settlement, and the Kotanigahora Kofun Cluster; however, neither of these sites received government protection and were totally destroyed during highway construction after a hasty {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|WD=Q653838|lt=compliance excavation|1=Rescue archaeology}}. The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were opened. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Tsuruga Station|en|Tsuruga Station|WD=Q5364670|lt=Tsuruga Station|1=Tsuruga Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|WD=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line|1=Hokuriku Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large Kofun from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Keyhole-shaped tumulus|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205293 |title= 中郷古墳群 |trans-title= Nakago kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Nakagō Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside east of the centre of the city of Tsuruga facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|qid=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}}, and consists of two separate groups: the {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster'''|向出山古墳群|Mukaiyama Kofun-gun}} and the {{nihongo|'''Myōjinyama Kofun Cluster'''|明神山古墳群|Myōjinyama Kofun-gun}}. These tumuli date from the 4th to 6th century AD and were discovered during the construction of a highway bypass connecting the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|en|Hokuriku Expressway|qid=Q1549512|lt=Hokuriku Expressway|1=Hokuriku Expressway}} with {{ill|Japan National Route 8|en|Japan National Route 8|qid=Q640239|lt=Japan National Route 8|1=Japan National Route 8}}, during which time a number of other archaeological discoveries were made, including the Yoshikawa Site, a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} settlement, and the Kotanigahora Kofun Cluster; however, neither of these sites received government protection and were totally destroyed during highway construction after a hasty {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|qid=Q653838|lt=compliance excavation|1=Rescue archaeology}}. The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were opened. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Tsuruga Station|en|Tsuruga Station|qid=Q5364670|lt=Tsuruga Station|1=Tsuruga Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|qid=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line|1=Hokuriku Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large Kofun from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Keyhole-shaped tumulus|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|qid=Q10222682|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)}}

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1988.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205293 |title= 中郷古墳群 |trans-title= Nakago kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Nakagō Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside east of the centre of the city of Tsuruga facing the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|qid=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}}, and consists of two separate groups: the {{nihongo|'''Mukaiyama Kofun Cluster'''|向出山古墳群|Mukaiyama Kofun-gun}} and the {{nihongo|'''Myōjinyama Kofun Cluster'''|明神山古墳群|Myōjinyama Kofun-gun}}. These tumuli date from the 4th to 6th century AD and were discovered during the construction of a highway bypass connecting the {{ill|Hokuriku Expressway|en|Hokuriku Expressway|qid=Q1549512|lt=Hokuriku Expressway|1=Hokuriku Expressway}} with {{ill|Japan National Route 8|en|Japan National Route 8|qid=Q640239|lt=Japan National Route 8|1=Japan National Route 8}}, during which time a number of other archaeological discoveries were made, including the Yoshikawa Site, a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} settlement, and the Kotanigahora Kofun Cluster; however, neither of these sites received government protection and were totally destroyed during highway construction after a hasty {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|qid=Q653838|lt=compliance excavation|1=Rescue archaeology}}. The Nakagō Kofun Cluster itself did not receive full protection, and within the site only Mukaiyama Tombs 1, 3 and 4 and Myōjinyama Tombs 1, 2, 3, 9 and 10 are covered by the National Historic Site designation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mukaiyama Tomb 1 was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times starting in 1954, and the two vertical-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were opened. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} found include weapons such as swords, spearheads, and fragments of gold-plated armor and helmets, {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, and agricultural implements.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The weapons in particular are considered to be rare examples nationwide, and have been preserved since 2010 at a private folk museum in Tsuruga.
The tumuli are about 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Tsuruga Station|en|Tsuruga Station|qid=Q5364670|lt=Tsuruga Station|1=Tsuruga Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|qid=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line|1=Hokuriku Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Mukaiyama site== This site consists of three surviving {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) on a hilltop ridge in the Yoshikawa neighborhood of Tsuruga. There is an explanatory plaque in front of each tumulus.
*Mukaiyama No.1: A large Kofun from the end of the fifth century, diameter 60 meters, height nine meters in two stages, faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. There is also a possibility that it was originally a scallop-shaped tumulus with a total length of 75 meters. There are two pit-type stone chambers at the top of the mound, and a large number of burial items have been excavated. However, the south side is partly missing due to construction work. *Mukaiyama No.2: This was a circular tumulus from the 5th century, possibility older than Mukaiyama No. 1. It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Keyhole-shaped tumulus|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

It was demolished during the construction work of 1983. *Mukaiyama No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, with a diameter of 15 meters, a height of four meters, with a corridor-type stone chamber (total length 7.2 meters, length of the entry room 3.7 meters, width of 2.0 meters, height 2.7 meters). *Mukaiyama No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the second half of the sixth century, diameter eight meters, height two meters, partially collapsed due to overgrowth of trees and plants.
== Myōjinyama site== This site consisted of 23 ''kofun'', of which five survive, on a hilltop ridge in the Sakanoshita neighborhood of Tsuruga. The site does not have any pathways or placards.
* Myōjinyama No.1: Keyhole-shaped tumulus ({{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|1=[[Zenpōkōenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}) from the 4th century, length 47 meters, height 5.5 meters, faced with ''fukiishi'' stones. * Myōjinyama No.2: {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=Circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} from the 4th century * Myōjinyama No.3: {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Keyhole-shaped tumulus|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} from the 4th century, length 53.5 meters, height seven meters * Myōjinyama No 9: A {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} from the first half of the sixth century, a length of 20 meters, a height of three meters, with a lateral hole type stone chamber * Myōjinyama No.10: A {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|qid=Q10222682|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mukaideyama Kofun Group}} *[http://www.city.tsuruga.lg.jp/about_city/news_from_facility/gaibu_shisetsu/hakubutsukan/past_exhibactivities/kaisetusito.files/001-bs02-mukaideyama.pdf Tsuruga city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/tsuruga-nakago.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}
